title: Secret Exposure Premerge Blocking
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: secret exposure premerge blocking.
assumptions: CI system integrates with configured secret scanners and can bind scanner profiles to PRs.
Reviewer capacity is represented by short-lived slots that can be consumed and released.
Automated runners can produce attestations that are trusted by the gate evaluation logic.
Policies and ownership metadata exist and are authoritative for routing reviews and approvals.
Secret scanners have acceptable detection coverage though false positives are possible and handled.
risks: False positives from secret scanners may block legitimate changes and increase cycle time.
Insufficient reviewer capacity can create bottlenecks and delay merges.
Adversarial obfuscation can evade rule-based secret detection, allowing secrets to slip through.
Overly strict gating may drive developers to bypass controls, increasing security risk.
Misconfiguration of scanner profiles or approval channels can produce orphaned PRs that never progress.
